name: ground_plane
role: World ground plane / floor collision; PhysX kinematic plane that catches anything dropped

gazebo.world_pose_source: /home/aaugus11/Documents/aic/aic_description/world/aic.sdf line 201 (<include><uri>model://Floor</uri></include> — no <pose> override, defaults to world origin)
gazebo.visual_uri: floor_visual.glb (2x2 unit plane, z=0) + walls_visual.glb (3D walls, sized 2 x 7.05 x 2 m — these are the room walls baked into 'Floor' model)
gazebo.collision_type: <plane size='10 10'/> (10x10m infinite plane proxy)
gazebo.scoring_references: off_limit_floor topics (touching the floor is a CheatCode penalty per aic_engine scoring); no per-frame TF

isaacsim.scale_op: size=5000 (5km x 5km) — implicit scaling on GroundPlane()
isaacsim.known_divergences: Gazebo Floor model includes a 'walls_visual.glb' room shell (3D walls forming an interior room) that the Isaac Sim version DOES NOT spawn separately — only the bare GroundPlane is created. The decorative room walls visible in Gazebo are absent in Isaac Sim. Also: GroundPlane is the default Isaac checkerboard, not the Gazebo concrete-floor texture.

notes: Floor model in Gazebo conflates two roles: collision plane + decorative room walls. Isaac Sim only models the collision plane.

name: floor_walls_decorative
role: Decorative 3D room walls baked into 'Floor' model (NOT collision)

gazebo.world_pose_source: Same Floor model as ground_plane — child visual of floor_link, no separate pose
gazebo.collision_type: (none — visual-only)

isaacsim.known_divergences: MISSING — not imported into Isaac Sim. The Gazebo room appearance comes from this, plus the Enclosure on top.

notes: Gazebo only; Isaac Sim relies solely on AIC_Enclosure for room context.

name: aic_enclosure
role: Workcell enclosure: floor + 4 corner posts + ceiling — physical bounding box for the robot/task-board scene

gazebo.world_pose_source: /home/aaugus11/Documents/aic/aic_description/world/aic.sdf line 188 (no <pose> override, identity)
gazebo.visual_uri: enclosure_visual.glb + light_visual.glb (a glowing ceiling diffuser plate)
gazebo.collision_type: 5x <box>: 1.5x1.139x1.5 floor box at z=0.57 (acts as floor extension), four 0.0508x0.0508x2.5m vertical corner posts at (±0.713, ±0.733, z=1.33), 0.0608x1.5x1.5 ceiling box at z=2.55
gazebo.scoring_references: Off-limit contact target — enclosure walls/posts are off_limit_static; CheatCode penalized if robot hits them

usd_asset_paths: exts/aic-dt/assets/scene/aic.usd (vendored compound USD of full enclosure)
isaacsim.known_divergences: enclosure_visual.glb POSITION buffer reports coords up to 2488 units — looks like raw mm packed as meter values (1500x2579x1500 'meters' is implausible for a workcell that should be ~1.5x1.5x2.5m). Either the GLB is in mm, OR the scene/aic.usd vendored asset has been pre-scaled in USD. Origin matches (0,0,0) on both sides.

notes: enclosurewalls_visual.glb (in same dir) is in meters (2x2x1.38m) and is the cleanly-scaled variant; main enclosure_visual.glb appears raw-mm. The Enclosure Walls sibling model is the one referenced as 'enclosure walls' include in aic.sdf line 195.

name: enclosure_walls
role: Glass/plexi side walls separate from the structural Enclosure — included as second model in world

gazebo.world_pose_source: /home/aaugus11/Documents/aic/aic_description/world/aic.sdf line 194-198
gazebo.collision_type: 4x <box>: 0.050x1.359x1.378 m vertical wall slabs at four sides (back/left/front/right walls of the workcell at z≈1.83m)
gazebo.scoring_references: Off-limit contact target (transparent wall hits penalized)

isaacsim.known_divergences: POSSIBLY ROLLED INTO scene/aic.usd — the enclosure USD in Isaac may bake both Enclosure and Enclosure Walls into a single asset. No separate /World/EnclosureWalls prim seen in the live probe. Cannot verify without inspecting aic.usd contents. If absent, transparent-wall collisions are not modeled in sim.

notes: Visual GLB also reports 1500-unit (mm-as-m) sizes. Collisions in model.sdf authored in meters and are correct.

name: dome_light
role: Ambient sky light for global illumination

gazebo.world_pose_source: aic.sdf lines 138-186 (spot enclosure_light + two point ceiling lights)
gazebo.collision_type: (none)

isaacsim.known_divergences: Gazebo uses three discrete spot/point lights with calibrated attenuation; Isaac Sim uses a single dome light at intensity 1000. Lighting falloff and shadows differ — this affects rendered camera images for vision-based policies.

notes: randomize_lighting() exists in extension.py and randomizes parameters at quick_start time

name: task_board_base
role: Base plate that mounts to the bench and carries all task-board children (rails, ports, NIC mounts)

gazebo.world_pose_source: /home/aaugus11/Documents/aic/aic_description/urdf/task_board.urdf.xacro lines 12-17 (xacro args x=-0.2 y=0.2 z=1.14 yaw=-3.141) — overridden per-trial by sample_config.yaml trials.<id>.scene.task_board.pose
gazebo.scoring_references: /scoring/tf publishes task_board/base_link frame; off_limit collisions check against the slab volume

extension_code_paths: /home/aaugus11/Documents/isaac-sim-mcp/exts/aic-dt/aic_dt/extension.py: _cmd_spawn_task_board_base @ line 4085 (defaults x=0.15 y=-0.2 z=1.14 yaw=3.1415 — trial_1/2 defaults, NOT xacro)
isaacsim.known_divergences: GLB AABB shows 21x322x440 — UNITS ARE MILLIMETERS (a real task board base is ~14x32x44 cm). The vendored task_board_rigid.usd / base_visual.usd were converted from this GLB; if the converter assumed meters, the on-disk USD would be 21x322x440 METERS (1000x too big). Audit note in extension.py line 4060 (taskboard-prim-authoring) suggests this is being handled via RigidBodyAPI kinematic_enabled fixups but does NOT mention a scale fix here. Live world pose matches Gazebo trial_1 (0.15, -0.2, 1.14). Default yaw in code is 3.1415 (== π); Gazebo xacro default is -3.141; sample_config.yaml trial_1 uses 3.1415. Match.

notes: Trials randomize translation/rotation of children (rails, ports, NIC mounts) but the base plate itself is at the trial-supplied pose.

name: nic_card_mount_0
role: NIC card mount rail (index 0/4) — a vertical bracket on the right side of the task board that holds a NIC card PCB. Has two sfp_port child sockets the cable plug must insert into.

gazebo.world_pose_source: /home/aaugus11/Documents/aic/aic_description/urdf/task_board.urdf.xacro line ~203 (anchor pose: x=-0.081418+translation, y=-0.1745, z=0.012 relative to base)
gazebo.collision_type: Mount link (nic_card_mount_link): 7+ box colliders forming the bracket geometry. NIC PCB link (nic_card_link): 8+ box colliders + 2 cylinder mounting-hole bushings. Additional sfp_port_0_link + sfp_port_1_link children with contact_collision boxes for plug insertion detection.
gazebo.scoring_references: /scoring/tf publishes task_board/nic_card_mount_0/nic_card_mount_link and child frames including sfp_port_0_link, sfp_port_1_link. anchor_target_offsets.yaml lists nic_card_mount anchor → sfp_port_0 transform (translation_xyz_m: 0.01095, -0.012865, 0.121476).

extension_code_paths: /home/aaugus11/Documents/isaac-sim-mcp/exts/aic-dt/aic_dt/extension.py: nic_card_link composition inline @ lines 4288-4324 (with 0.01x scale fix per nic-card-visual-scale comment)
usd_asset_paths: exts/aic-dt/assets/assets/NIC Card Mount/nic_card_mount_visual.usd (THE BRACKET — but per extension comment '...ships as ONLY a single ISO 4762 M2x8 screw mesh — no mount body, no PCB')
exts/aic-dt/assets/assets/NIC Card Mount/nic_card_visual.usd (PCB — vertex coords are 100x too big per inline comment)
exts/aic-dt/assets/assets/NIC Card Mount/nic_card_mount_visual_v2.usd (alternate v2 — purpose not documented)
isaacsim.scale_op: child nic_card_link gets Scale(0.01, 0.01, 0.01) because nic_card_visual.usd vertices are 100x too big (per extension.py comment line ~4306)
isaacsim.known_divergences: MAJOR: (1) The vendored nic_card_mount_visual.usd reportedly contains ONLY an M2x8 screw mesh — the actual bracket geometry is MISSING from the Isaac Sim asset. Comment at line 4288 acknowledges this and composes a separate nic_card_link child to add the PCB, but the BRACKET BODY itself appears not to render properly. (2) The PCB child (nic_card_visual.usd) has vertex coords 100x too big and needs an inline Scale(0.01) workaround. (3) The 10+ collision boxes from Gazebo's nic_card_mount_link are NOT replicated; Isaac Sim authors only two sphere colliders for sfp_port_0/1 contact detection. (4) sfp_port_0 collider offset (0.01095, -0.012865, 0.121476) matches anchor_target_offsets.yaml exactly; sfp_port_1 offset (-0.01225, ...) is DERIVED from SDF delta — not directly verified against the live Gazebo TF.

notes: index 0: currently active in live scene

name: sc_port_0
role: SC fiber-optic port (index 0/1) — a flush rectangular socket on the task board for SC plug insertion

gazebo.world_pose_source: /home/aaugus11/Documents/aic/aic_description/urdf/task_board.urdf.xacro lines 180-188 (anchor: x=-0.075+translation, y=0.0295, z=0.0165, rpy=(1.57+roll, pitch, 1.57+yaw))
gazebo.collision_type: 8+ box colliders + cylinder; including sc_port_base_link child link for the actual insertion socket cavity. Surface friction tuned (torsional 0.5, bullet 0.5).
gazebo.scoring_references: /scoring/tf publishes task_board/sc_port_0/sc_port_link AND sc_port_base_link. anchor_target_offsets.yaml lists sc_port anchor → sc_port_base transform (translation 0, -0.002, 0).

isaacsim.known_divergences: GLB AABB 15.0 x 23.3 x 19.5 — likely mm-as-m (real SC port is ~15mm x 23mm x 19mm — these numbers match if interpreted as mm). The vendored sc_port.usd was converted; whether the converter unit-normalized is unverified. Index 0 active. Collision footprint differs from Gazebo: Gazebo has 8+ boxes including a sc_port_base_link child for the actual receptacle; Isaac Sim uses whatever fell out of the USD convert.

notes: index 0: currently active in live scene

name: nic_card_link_pcb

(2) The 100x scale bug on nic_card_visual.usd is being worked around inline — fragile. (3) No collision authoring — only the bare USD reference, no UsdPhysics.CollisionAPI applied. The Gazebo PCB has 8+ collision shapes. (4) Pose vs Gazebo: (-0.002, -0.01785, 0.0899) rpy=(-1.57,0,0) is reproduced correctly per the extension.py code.

notes: This is the 'disconnected PCB' the user mentioned — the bracket geometry is missing from the USD, so the PCB appears to float relative to the rail.

name: sfp_port_0_collider
role: Insertion-target sphere collider for SFP plug into NIC card port (index 0 — closer to outside edge of NIC card). The 'goal' for CheatCode.

gazebo.world_pose_source: NIC Card Mount/model.sdf: sfp_port_0_link relative to nic_card_link at (0.01295, -0.031572, 0.00501); Gazebo TouchPlugin uses contact_collision boxes at (0.012963, -0.0305, 0.002845).
gazebo.default_pose: Per anchor_target_offsets.yaml: anchor→port translation_xyz_m=(0.01095, -0.012865, 0.121476) relative to nic_card_mount_link
gazebo.collision_type: TouchPlugin contact_collision_01 + _02 boxes — 8mm x 0.25mm contact sensors
gazebo.scoring_references: /scoring/tf publishes task_board/nic_card_mount_<i>/sfp_port_0_link. /scoring/insertion_event fires on plug-in-port contact. CheatCode policy targets this frame.

extension_code_paths: /home/aaugus11/Documents/isaac-sim-mcp/exts/aic-dt/aic_dt/extension.py: inline authoring in _cmd_spawn_nic_card_mount @ lines 4221-4286 (port_offsets dict line 4266)
isaacsim.known_divergences: (1) Replaces Gazebo's 8x0.25mm contact box pair with a single 12mm sphere — more generous, easier for CheatCode to hit, but does not match Gazebo's contact dynamics exactly. (2) Offset uses exact anchor_target_offsets.yaml value (0.01095, -0.012865, 0.121476) which is the CV-detected runtime offset — that's the right number for scoring parity. (3) Critical bug-fix comment in code: DO NOT set Purpose=guide on the sphere (would silently exclude from PhysX contact discovery).

notes: CheatCode plugs INTO this. Insertion event fires when plug_proxy sphere overlaps this sphere.

extension_code_paths: /home/aaugus11/Documents/isaac-sim-mcp/exts/aic-dt/aic_dt/extension.py: D-09 note: 'Hand-E finger joint is a FixedJoint zero-DOF in our USD' @ line 1627
isaacsim.known_divergences: MAJOR: per D-09, the Hand-E fingers are authored as a FixedJoint with zero DOF in the unified USD — the gripper does NOT actually open/close in sim. /gripper_command parameter is recorded for parity but has no physical effect. Gazebo gripper has 2 prismatic finger joints. Cable attachment uses plug_proxy hierarchy trick instead of physical grasping. hande_finger_visual.glb AABB 4.52x3.14x6.19 indicates the GLB is in mm (real finger is ~45x31x62 mm).

notes: The gripper is essentially a static appendage carrying the plug_proxy. Real grasping is faked via plug_proxy USD-hierarchy parenting.

The F/T sensor isn't actually measuring contact forces — the OmniGraph node may need a contact-aware sensor binding. GLB AABB 8.2x9.12x2.54 is in mm (real Axia80 is ~80x91x25mm).

notes: Critical for impedance control and insertion-force scoring. Currently broken per Phase 1 audit.

isaacsim.known_divergences: Pose at probe time (-0.20, 0.1846, 1.1825) — note this is RELATIVE to /World/UR5e parent xform which is at (-0.20, 0.20, 1.14), so cable world position = (-0.40, 0.385, 2.3) [approx — depends on rotation]. Live shows world translate (-0.2, 0.1846, 1.1825) meaning cable's WORLD translation matches the UR5e base location, NOT the gazebo-specified (0.172, 0.024, 1.518) world. Local offset (0, 0.0154, 0.0425) per gripper_offset is being applied as the LOCAL transform under /World/UR5e — i.e. cable pose is gripper-relative not world-absolute. Cable mass authored at density=5e-5 per author_cable_physics_offline.py.

notes: The cable's WORLD pose is the result of UR5e parent xform composition + cable local offset, which is the GAZEBO-LIKE convention.

name: cable_rope_chain_21_links
role: 21-segment articulated rope chain (link_0 .. link_20) connecting cable_end_0 and cable_end_1. The physical 'rope'.

gazebo.world_pose_source: Each link's <pose> is a 6-vec relative to the previous. Joints are 'ball' type with stiffness=100M, damping=0.2.
gazebo.default_pose: link_1 starts at z=-0.024 relative to cable_end_0; each subsequent link offsets ~24mm down the cable axis
gazebo.visual_uri: (per-link sphere/cylinder visuals authored inline in cable_base_c_rotated/model.sdf)
gazebo.collision_type: Per-link sphere radius=0.002m (collision) + cylinder length=0.024m radius=0.002m. link_1 collision is OVERRIDDEN by sfp_sc_cable/model.sdf experimental:params (removed entirely to avoid gripper-palm intersect).

extension_code_paths: /home/aaugus11/Documents/isaac-sim-mcp/exts/aic-dt/aic_dt/extension.py: comment refs at line 1346 ('21-segment cable rope chain')
usd_asset_paths: Embedded in unified USD at /World/UR5e/cable/Rope/Rope/link_0 .. link_20
isaacsim.known_divergences: 21 links present (link_0 .. link_20 confirmed in live probe). PhysX simulation of the rope is currently fragile — per CLAUDE.md cable note, mass=0/inertia=0 means PhysX treats links as kinematic and they DO NOT FALL UNDER GRAVITY. Authoring per author_cable_physics_offline.py applies density=5e-5 per link (per code comment line 1376). Live probe shows link_20 at world (0.148, 0.671, 1.144) — far from the gripper end (~0.17, 0.0, 1.64), which means the rope is hanging unphysically across the workspace rather than being held by the gripper. The plug_proxy USD-hierarchy trick replaces real grasping.

notes: Two fixedJoints at /World/UR5e/cable/Rope/fixedJoint and /fixedJoint2 anchor the rope endpoints. Number of links per probe = exactly 21.

name: cable_sc_plug_visual
role: SC plug — one end of the cable. The 'female' connector that mates with sc_port_base. Visual-only in Isaac Sim under cable/sc_plug_visual.

gazebo.world_pose_source: sfp_sc_cable/model.sdf: <include><pose relative_to='cable_connection_1'>0.052 0 0 0 0 0.0</pose></include>
gazebo.collision_type: 10+ box colliders forming the plug housing; some removed by sfp_sc_cable/model.sdf experimental:params (housing-vs-gripper intersect workaround)
gazebo.scoring_references: /scoring/tf cable/sc_plug_link; trial_3 task targets sc_tip plug into sc_port_base

usd_asset_paths: exts/aic-dt/assets/assets/SC Plug/sc_plug_visual.usd; embedded in cable subtree of unified USD
isaacsim.scale_op: (identity — GLB is in METERS, AABB 0.057x0.025x0.010 is correct for a real SC plug)
isaacsim.known_divergences: Per code comment line 1547-1562: cross-articulation FixedJoint between sc_plug_visual and gripper finger CANNOT work (different articulation roots). sc_plug_visual is DECORATIVE in the cable subtree — it does not move with the gripper. The plug_proxy sphere child of finger_link_l is the actual collision body for scoring. sc_plug_visual drifts ~30mm under gripper motion because cable density=5e-5 has near-zero gravity coupling.

notes: The user's mental model 'gripper holds plug, plug inserts into port' is broken by USD physics constraints. plug_proxy is the workaround.

name: cable_lc_plug_visual
role: LC plug — combined LC plug (small fiber connector) bundled with SFP module on the other end of the cable from sc_plug

gazebo.world_pose_source: sfp_sc_cable/model.sdf: <include><pose relative_to='cable_connection_0'>-0.041 0 0 0 0 1.57079</pose></include>
gazebo.collision_type: 10+ box+cylinder colliders; 6 of them REMOVED by sfp_sc_cable/model.sdf experimental:params (gripper finger intersect)

usd_asset_paths: Embedded in cable subtree at /World/UR5e/cable/sfp_module_visual/lc_plug_visual (NESTED under sfp_module_visual per live probe)
isaacsim.known_divergences: USD hierarchy nests lc_plug_visual UNDER sfp_module_visual — opposite of Gazebo where SFP Module is the child of LC Plug (per sfp_sc_cable/model.sdf line 'pose relative_to=lc_plug_link' on SFP Module include).

notes: The SFP+LC plug combo is the 'sfp_tip' that CheatCode inserts into nic_card_mount/sfp_port_0 (trial_1/2).

name: cable_sfp_module_visual
role: SFP module — the transceiver that mates with sfp_port on the NIC card. Sibling of lc_plug.

gazebo.world_pose_source: sfp_sc_cable/model.sdf: <include><pose relative_to='lc_plug_link'>0 0.0384 0 0 3.14159 3.14159</pose></include>
gazebo.default_pose: (0, 0.0384, 0) rpy=(0, π, π) relative to lc_plug_link
gazebo.collision_type: 15+ box colliders for housing, latch, port, head — ALL REMOVED by sfp_sc_cable/model.sdf experimental:params (gripper interference)

isaacsim.known_divergences: Live probe shows /World/UR5e/cable/sfp_module_visual has lc_plug_visual as a CHILD (inverted hierarchy vs Gazebo). All Gazebo collision boxes are REMOVED in cable.sdf.xacro experimental:params anyway, so divergence is moot for physics. Also: orphan FixedJoint at /World/UR5e/aic_unified_robot/gripper_hande_finger_link_r/FixedJoint formerly pointed to /World/UR5e/cable/sfp_module_visual — cleanup removes the phantom prim per code line 1605-1625.

notes: The 'plug' the gripper is supposed to hold. Replaced by plug_proxy for scoring.

name: plug_proxy
role: ISAAC SIM ONLY — kinematic USD-hierarchy stand-in for the cable plug under the gripper finger. Provides the collision body that scoring uses for /scoring/insertion_event detection. Replaces cross-articulation FixedJoint which PhysX cannot cook.

gazebo.world_pose_source: (N/A — does not exist in Gazebo)
gazebo.scoring_references: (N/A in Gazebo — Gazebo uses CablePlugin + real fixedjoint)

isaacsim.known_divergences: ISAAC-ONLY ARCHITECTURAL ADDITION. Created at finger_link_l with zero local offset; gets the finger's world pose every frame for free via USD hierarchy composition. UsdPhysics.CollisionAPI applied to a sphere child. Code comment WARNS not to set Purpose=guide (silently excludes from PhysX contact discovery — broke trial_2 in iter 6). Gazebo equivalent is the CablePlugin + cross-body FixedJoint, which CANNOT be authored in Isaac without crossing articulation roots.

notes: The 'cheat': the gripper is its own plug for scoring purposes. The real cable visual is decorative.

name: cable_attach_joint
role: In Gazebo: CablePlugin authors a real FixedJoint connecting cable plug-end link to gripper. In Isaac Sim: this joint is INTENTIONALLY NOT AUTHORED — replaced by plug_proxy hierarchy approach.

gazebo.world_pose_source: Runtime — CablePlugin creates joint at sim startup when attach_cable_to_gripper=true
gazebo.scoring_references: Allows cable plug links to track gripper world pose for /scoring/tf

extension_code_paths: /home/aaugus11/Documents/isaac-sim-mcp/exts/aic-dt/aic_dt/extension.py: _cmd_attach_cable_to_gripper @ line 3327 — comment block at 1530+ explains why FixedJoint approach was abandoned
isaacsim.known_divergences: ARCHITECTURAL DIVERGENCE: cross-articulation FixedJoint between /World/UR5e/cable/Rope/Rope/link_20 and /World/UR5e/aic_unified_robot/gripper_hande_finger_link_l wedges PhysX reduced-coordinate solver because it crosses articulation roots. excludeFromArticulation crashes during cook. excludeFromArticulation=False + localPos0/1=0 wedges asyncio. The 'Path (a') USD-hierarchy plug proxy' (Milestone 1 ship commit a31b3a1) is the chosen workaround.

notes: See git log a31b3a1 'M1 SHIPPED — all 3 trials pass' — the resolution that closed Milestone 1.
